hooks_predicted: In this chapter of My China Diaries, come explore the beautiful Jiuzhaigou National Park with me.
It used to take about 8-10 hours from Chengdu by bus but with the opening of the railway station the whole journey has been cut down to around 4 hours in total.
Jiuzhaigou is located in the north of Sichuan province and has been recognised as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO since 1992.
It's most well known for its many waterfalls and colourful lakes which are especially stunning during autumn.
This is the biggest, largest and also highest lake in Jiuzhaigou being at an altitude of 3,150 metres.
In local folklore it's said that there's a monster in the lake and there's an old man with one arm who guards the lake.
From this point it's a 17km walk until the next bus stop
Fortunately the entire scenic area is paved throughout with about 70km of really well maintained boardwalks
In many ways I do think Jiuzhaigou brings together the worst and the best parts of travelling in China.
selling_points_predicted: UNESCO World Heritage Site with international recognition since 1992
Journey time reduced to around 4 hours total with newly opened high-speed railway connection
Famous for many waterfalls and colourful lakes that are especially stunning during autumn
Convenient ticket purchasing for foreign travellers through trip.com using passport
Extensive 70km of well-maintained boardwalks throughout the entire scenic area
Long Lake is the biggest, largest and highest lake in Jiuzhaigou at 3,150 metres altitude
Public toilets and rest points selling snacks at reasonable prices throughout the park
Five Flower Lake showcases various colours and is one of the centrepieces of the scenic area
Pearl Beach featured in the iconic 1986 version of Journey to the West television series
Great escape from relentless heat experienced in Chengdu and Chongqing
Showcases China's abundant natural beauty outside of its major cities
Well-run and well-maintained scenic area with significant time and resources invested
